summary:
The main reason endodontically treated posterior teeth have a greater risk of fractures due to loss of pulpal tissue leading to decreased structural integrity; additional vulnerability can arise from procedural technique choices affecting remaining dental structure negatively post-endodontics treatment completion. To summarize Agent2’s core rationale succinctly while adhering strictly within two-sentence constraints as requested without extraneous details:

Endodontically treated molars tend to break easier owing primarily to lost inner supportive tissues weakening overall resilience. Further fragility may result based on specific surgical methods impacting what remains after intervention adversely.
